title: FormalJets
summary: Defines a purely formal expression language for metric jets, Taylor denominators, and obstruction coefficients.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines a purely formal expression language for metric jets, Taylor denominators, and obstruction coefficients.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal boundary
mathematicalContent.body: This page describes exactly the declarations present in Juhlformula/Juhlcombinatorics/FormalJets.lean; claims not represented by a declaration are not counted as verified.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: Formal Expr. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: qsmul. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: fact Q. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: inv Fact Q. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: inv Fact2 Q. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: list Sum. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def listSum : List FormalExpr → FormalExpr | [] => 0 | x :: xs => x + listSum xs

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: range Sum. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def rangeSum (m : ℕ) (A : ℕ → FormalExpr) : FormalExpr

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: highest Coeff. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: trace Derivative Part. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def traceDerivativePart : ℕ → FormalExpr | 0 => 0 | m' + 1 => rangeSum m' fun j => FormalExpr.qsmul (2 * invFact2Q j (m' - j)) ((FormalExpr.tr j) * (FormalExpr.F (m' + 1 - j)))

limitations: Verified means that Lean checks the propositions encoded in this file and their imported assumptions. It does not assert that the present formal statement exhausts every analytic hypothesis or convention in the cited literature.
